scenario.name: boots_into_textured_rotating_mode7_arena
scenario.description: Under RANDOM power-on the ROM boots ($7E:E000=SFDB) into a TEXTURED rotating Mode 7 overhead-shooter arena: the floor is the Mode 7 BG (one uniform affine matrix), the hero is an OBJ pinned at screen centre. The hero (OAM slot 0, tile 0) renders screen-centred at (120,104) — a 16x16 sprite centred on screen (128,112) — and UPRIGHT (attr 0x20, no H/V flip; the affine matrix never touches OBJ). A screenshot blob (>=4 distinct colours) proves a REAL TEXTURED floor rendered (blue checker + terracotta wall ring + pillar cover), not a flat fill or a black band. The heartbeat at $E010 advances (the 60fps game loop is live). Reads the rendered framebuffer + OAM bytes, never a proxy.

scenario.name: eight_way_input_rotates_the_rendered_floor
scenario.description: 8-WAY aim/move (model A): holding a D-pad direction snaps R_ANGLE to its compass heading, which drives the floor matrix so the player's facing reads 'up' and the floor visibly ROTATES. Unlike an animated floor, this static-affine plane does not move without input, so a before/after screenshot diff under a turn is a TRUE rotation signal. axis_sweep holds LEFT then DOWN (each a large heading change from boot heading 0), captures before/after, and screenshot_changed asserts >=20% of the floor pixels changed for BOTH. The hero stays screen-centred at (120,104). Reads the rendered framebuffer + OAM.

scenario.name: fired_bullet_renders_yellow_on_the_floor
scenario.description: Fire (A, rising edge) spawns a bullet at the hero world pos with a velocity along the facing; the bullet is PROJECTED onto the rotating floor via the transpose matrix (the same path the enemies use) and DRAWS in its own bright-yellow palette (bullet_pal[1]=(255,230,40)). The 'yellow' predicate (r>150,g>150,b<90) matches the bullet bolt but REJECTS the red enemy and the cyan hero and the floor. A script fires one bullet and lets it travel a few frames; >=10 bullet-yellow pixels render on the framebuffer (an invisible / wrong-palette / mis-projected bullet yields 0). Reads RENDERED PIXELS. (The per-angle rendered-floor binding guard + the -DBULLET_PROJ_FORWARD non-vacuity control live in tests/test_m7_oshoot.py.)

scenario.name: enemy_wave_chaser_renders_red_on_the_floor
scenario.description: Timed enemy WAVES (sf_pool, shmup cadence) spawn chasers at a world ring around the player; each is projected onto the rotating floor and DRAWS in its red palette (enemy_pal[1]=(200,30,30)). After ~3 wave periods (settle 180f) at least one chaser is on screen and renders red. The 'red' predicate (r>150,g<90,b<90) matches the enemy body but rejects the floor + the cyan hero + the yellow bullet, so a count of red pixels is enemy-specific. >=20 enemy-red pixels render on the framebuffer (an invisible / off-screen / mis-projected chaser yields 0). Reads RENDERED PIXELS.
